channel.title: InnoTep Research Group
channel.description: Recent content on InnoTep Research Group

item.title: María Dolón and Fernando Díez, new doctors in the group
item.description: María Dolón Poza and Fernando Díez Muñoz have defended their doctoral theses and join InnoTep as new doctors.

item.title: Neuro-Rebuild
item.description: European project (Horizon Europe) coordinated by UPM-ETSIST, with a 10-partner consortium and a €3.35M budget, devoted to neuro-responsive urban reconstruction for inclusive and sustainable environments (Sept 2026 – Aug 2029).

item.title: New JMIR publication on data poisoning in health care AI
item.description: InnoTep publishes in the Journal of Medical Internet Research a security framework and defense strategies against data poisoning in health care AI architectures.

item.title: Data Poisoning Vulnerabilities Across Health Care Artificial Intelligence Architectures: Analytical Security Framework and Defense Strategies
Welcome to Cristóbal Tapia García
item.description: Since January 2026, the InnoTep group welcomes Dr. Cristóbal Tapia García as Assistant Professor at ETSIS de Telecomunicación.

item.title: Leveraging Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Automated Smart Home Orchestration
Proyecto de Aprendizaje-Servicio (ApS) - GADES
item.description: This project aims to validate and improve Gades: a technological solution to support education professionals in identifying language problems in children, a website for assessing language and communication in children aged 0 to 6, through the collaboration of an undergraduate student.

item.title: Proposal for a security and privacy enhancement system for private smart environments
Enhancing Early Language Disorder Detection in Preschools: Evaluation and Future Directions for the Gades Platform
Smart Textile Technology for the Monitoring of Mental Health
Enhancing Preschool Language Acquisition through Robotic Assistants: An Evaluation of Effectiveness, Engagement, and Acceptance
Welcome to Mª Cristina Guerrero-García
item.description: The InnoTep team welcomes Mª Cristina Guerrero-García, Assistant Professor (Profesora Ayudante Doctora) of the ETSIS de Telecomunicación

item.title: New publication on security vulnerabilities in healthcare AI
item.description: InnoTep researchers publish comprehensive analysis of data poisoning threats in healthcare AI systems

item.title: DataWave - An interactive and visual tool for creating and exploring large knowledge bases
Analysis of security and privacy issues in wearables for minors
Certified Innovation Path: FELL Programme
item.description: Development of courses on cybersecurity in the field of digital health and clinical data.

item.title: Future of ADHD Care: Evaluating the Efficacy of ChatGPT in Therapy Enhancement
PLATA
item.description: The objective of the PLATA project is to develop educational prototypes to digitise the editorial collection of the UDIT Student Residence so that it can be used as teaching material in secondary schools, sixth form colleges and universities, both for classroom use and for independent research work at all levels, including final degree projects and doctoral theses. The project offers the opportunity to digitise the editorial collection in a modern and durable interactive format in line with the current uses of new generations, facilitating consultation and reading through interactive technologies, cloud file consultation and artificial intelligence.

item.title: SmartDiabetes
item.description: SMARTDIABETES solution enhances diabetes care through a data-driven digital ecosystem. It integrates the SocialDiabetes tool for patient self-management and remote monitoring across three clinical sites. The project creates a value-based healthcare reimbursement model and delivers a comprehensive operating blueprint for healthcare organizations, including care pathways, user support, and training strategies. By prioritizing adoption, acceptance, and sustainability, SMARTDIABETES puts patients and care providers at the center, ensuring long-term impact through collaboration among clinical, academic, and business experts.

item.title: The Potential of Digital Screening Tools for Childhood ADHD in Scholl Environments: A Preliminary Study
In Unity, There Is Strength: On Weighted Voting Ensembles for Hurtful Humour Detection
Serious Game for the Screening of Central Auditory Processing Disorder in School-Aged Children: Development and Validation Study
A collaborative semantic framework based on activities for the development of applications in Smart Home living labs
A New Architecture for Customizable Exergames: User Evaluation for Different Neuromuscular Disorders
Robotic Technologies in ADHD Care: Literature Review
Assessment of a Robotic Assistant for Supporting Homework Activities of Children With ADHD
TEACH ME: A GAMING SOLUTION TO SUPPORT THE TREATMENT OF CHILDREN WITH AUTISM SPECTRUM DISORDER
item.description: Development of serious toys for therapy for children with autism

item.title: Creation of an intelligent system to support the therapy process in children with ADHD
Smart-Home Environment to Support Homework Activities for Children
Personalized Health and Intelligent Workplaces Transforming Ergonomics: Some Lessons Learnt
P-Ergonomics Platform: Toward precise, pervasive, and personalized ergonomics using wearable sensors and edge computing
Secure Communications and Protected Data for a Internet of Things Smart Toy Platform
Characterization of user-centered security in telehealth services
Outpatient readmission in rheumatology: a machine learning predictive model of patient’s return to the clinic
Transforming Ergonomics with Personalized Health and Intelligent Workplaces
Value
item.description: The VALUE solution helps turn digital health transformation into reality. Defeating patient disease and restoring patient health is the dominant concept driving healthcare delivery in most facilities. The growing prevalence of chronic diseases among the population is jeopardising the sustainability of the current healthcare system. VALUE is a solution aimed at healthcare providers to deliver healthcare services that provide value to patients while maintaining sustainable healthcare.

item.title: A different approach for digital pathology: Lexicon-semantic analysis of histopathological reports for the assessment of their quality
From general services to pervasive and sensitive services
Developing a System for Processing Health Data of Children Using Digitalized Toys: Ethical and Privacy Concerns for the Internet of Things Paradigm
A Smart Telecare System at Digital Home: Perceived Usefulness, Satisfaction, and Expectations for Healthcare Professionals
A Smart Toy to Enhance the Decision-Making Process at Children's Psychomotor Delay Screenings: A Pilot Study
An Innovative Serious Game for the Detection and Rehabilitation of Oral-Facial Malfunction in Children: A Pilot Study
The Role of Smart Homes in Intelligent Homecare and Healthcare Environments
Interaction patterns for smart spaces: A confident interaction design solution for pervasive sensitive IoT services
A Virtual Environment to Improve the Detection of Oral-Facial Malfunction in Children with Cerebral Palsy
Adaptive software architecture based on confident HCI for the deployment of sensitive services in smart homes
Utilizing Smart Textiles-Enabled Sensorized Toy and Playful Interactions for Assessment of Psychomotor Development on Children
Ubiquitous Detection, Attention and Early Stimulation Ecosystem for Children with Developmental Disorders
item.description: Development of smart toys for detecting motor disorders in children.

item.title: Evaluating a Web-Based Clinical Decision Support System for Language Disorders Screening in a Nursery School
Deployment and Validation of a Smart System for Screening of Language Disorders in Primary Care
BSc Thesis: Audit System and Adversarial Injection Detection in Clinical AI
item.description:
General Description

The objective of this BSc thesis is to design and implement a “Security Gateway” for healthcare environments, capable of intercepting, analyzing, and auditing clinical text flows before they are processed by critical decision systems.

Motivation

The vulnerability of Large Language Models (LLMs) to data poisoning attacks represents a critical risk in healthcare environments. Small manipulations in text (indirect injections or “trigger” words) can force erroneous diagnoses or information leaks, remaining invisible to traditional quality controls.

item.title: Final Degree and Master's Thesis Projects
item.description:
Project Opportunities for Students

At InnoTep we offer Final Degree Projects (BSc Thesis) and Master’s Thesis (MSc Thesis) focused on technological innovation applied to the fields of health, security, privacy, and education.

Working with our group will allow you to participate in real projects with social impact, collaborate internationally, apply cutting-edge technologies, and receive specialized mentoring.

🎯 Available Projects

These are specific BSc/MSc thesis proposals you can develop with InnoTep. We are also open to student proposals that align with our research lines.

item.title: Innovation Management
item.description:
Short-term goals

  • Assess adoption barriers in real-world contexts.
  • Design training and deployment plans for innovative solutions.

Long-term goals

  • Establish replicable models of sustainability and scalability.
  • Promote cross-sector collaboration to maximize impact.

item.title: Innovative Therapies
item.description:
Short-term goals

  • Develop functional prototypes of personalized therapy devices.
  • Validate solutions with patients in real-world environments.

Long-term goals

  • Establish new data-driven and AI-based therapeutic methodologies.
  • Integrate our solutions into existing healthcare systems.

item.title: Interaction Systems
item.description:
Short-term goals

  • Design intuitive interfaces adapted to user capabilities.
  • Implement tracking and automated response systems in home and work environments.

Long-term goals

  • Create human-centered intelligent environments.
  • Contribute to standards of accessibility and technological usability.

item.title: Legal notice
item.description:
1. Identifying information

In accordance with Article 10 of Spanish Law 34/2002 on Information Society Services and Electronic Commerce (LSSI-CE), this website is owned by:

  • Owner: InnoTep research group.
  • Affiliation: School of Telecommunication Engineering and Systems (ETSIST), Universidad Politécnica de Madrid (UPM).
  • Address: ETSIS de Telecomunicación (UPM), Calle Nikola Tesla s/n, 28031 Madrid.
  • Contact email: gi.innotep@upm.es.

2. Purpose

This website is intended to disseminate the research activity, publications, projects and members of the InnoTep group. It is purely informational.

item.description:

item.title: Privacy and cookie policy
item.description:
1. Data controller

  • Controller: InnoTep research group — Universidad Politécnica de Madrid (UPM).
  • Contact email: gi.innotep@upm.es.
  • Data Protection Unit (UPM): proteccion.datos@upm.es.

2. What data we process

This website is informational and static. It has no contact forms, registration or e-commerce, it does not collect or store personal data from visitors directly, and it does not use any first-party analytics.

The only data that may be processed is that technically logged by the third-party services described in section 4 (for example, the IP address when loading external resources).

item.title: TUG — Trust, Uncertainty & Governance
item.description:
TUG studies and designs the relationship between people and opaque artificial intelligence systems, so that it stays functional, traceable and auditable. It treats trust as infrastructure: something that can be observed, measured and audited, not merely declared.

Purpose

When an organisation brings AI into its decisions, the question is no longer how well a model performs. It becomes what relationship a person maintains with a system they do not fully control.
